proofs.proof_tex: Let \(e_q^P(x)=\min\{1-q,\max\{q,e_P(x)\}\}\) and \(\widehat e_q(x)=\min\{1-q,\max\{q,\widehat e(x)\}\}\). The scalar clipping map \(z\mapsto \min\{1-q,\max\{q,z\}\}\) is 1-Lipschitz, hence \(|\widehat e_q-e_q^P|\le |\widehat e-e_P|\) pointwise and \(\|\widehat e_q-e_q^P\|_2\le \|\widehat e-e_P\|_2\). It remains to bound the true clipping displacement. If \(e_P(x)<q\), then \(p_P(x)=e_P(x)\le q\) and \(|e_q^P(x)-e_P(x)|=q-e_P(x)\le q\). If \(e_P(x)>1-q\), then \(p_P(x)=1-e_P(x)\le q\) and \(|e_q^P(x)-e_P(x)|=e_P(x)-(1-q)\le q\). Otherwise the displacement is zero. Therefore \(|e_q^P-e_P|\le q\,1\{p_P\le q\}\). For \(\gamma>0\), ass:marginal-overlap gives \(P_X\{p_P(X)\le q\}\le C_{om}q^{1/\gamma}\) for \(q\le c_o\), and increasing the constant handles the finite range \(q\in(c_o,1/2]\). Hence \(\|e_q^P-e_P\|_2\le q P_X\{p_P\le q\}^{1/2}\le C q^{1+1/(2\gamma)}\). The triangle inequality yields \(\|\widehat e_q-e_P\|_2\le \|\widehat e-e_P\|_2+Cq^{1+1/(2\gamma)}\). If \(\gamma=0\) and ass:strict-overlap-endpoint holds, then for every \(q\le \underline p\) the true propensity is never clipped, so \(e_q^P=e_P\) almost surely and \(\|\widehat e_q-e_P\|_2\le \|\widehat e-e_P\|_2\).

We prove the feasible upper bound using the corrected feasible-rate definition proposed below. Fix \(P\in P_{class}(\alpha,\gamma)\). Condition on the nuisance-training folds, so the cross-fitted nuisance estimates are fixed on the fold where the empirical policy value is evaluated. Write \(\widehat e_q\) for the clipped estimated propensity and \(b_q(x)=E_P[\Gamma_q(O;\widehat\eta)\mid X=x]-\tau_P(x)\). By lem:clip-bias, \(b_q=(\widehat e_q-e_P)(\Delta_1/\widehat e_q+\Delta_0/(1-\widehat e_q))\). Since \(q\le \widehat e_q\le 1-q\), lem:feasible-drift-global-bound gives the uniform drift scale \(B_n(q)=C\{r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}/q+1\{\gamma>0\}r_{\mu,n}q^{1/(2\gamma)}\}\), with the last term absent at \(\gamma=0\) when \(q\le\underline p\). The ERM inequality from def:feasible-erm gives \(P_n[(\pi_\star-\widehat\pi_n)\Gamma_q(\widehat\eta)]\le0\), hence \(R_P(\widehat\pi_n)\le (P-P_n)[(\pi_\star-\widehat\pi_n)\Gamma_q(\widehat\eta)]+|P[(\pi_\star-\widehat\pi_n)b_q]|\). By ass:vc-localized-envelope and the clipped-score second-moment bound \(E_P[\Gamma_q(O;\widehat\eta)^2 1_D(X)\mid \widehat\eta]\le Cq^{-1}P_X(D)\), the localized empirical term has expectation at most \(C n^{-1/2}q^{-1/2}P_X(D_{\widehat\pi_n})^{1/2}(\log n)^{p_0}\). By thm:margin-localization, \(P_X(D_{\widehat\pi_n})^{1/2}\le C R_P(\widehat\pi_n)^{\alpha/(2+2\alpha)}\). Thus, with \(A_n(q)=n^{-1/2}q^{-1/2}(\log n)^{p_0}\), \(s=\alpha/(2+2\alpha)\), and \(\kappa=1/(1-s)=2(1+\alpha)/(2+\alpha)\), the basic inequality is \(R_P(\widehat\pi_n)\le C A_n(q)R_P(\widehat\pi_n)^s+C B_n(q)\). The elementary inequality \(r\le ar^s+b\), \(0\le s<1\), implies \(r\le C_s(a^{1/(1-s)}+b)\); applying it gives \(E_P R_P(\widehat\pi_n)\le C\{[n^{-1/2}q^{-1/2}(\log n)^{p_0}]^\kappa+r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}/q+1\{\gamma>0\}r_{\mu,n}q^{1/(2\gamma)}\}\). This is lem:feasible-additive-master-bound. For \(\gamma>0\), assume polynomial nuisance certificates \(r_{\mu,n}=O(n^{-a})\) and \(r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}=O(n^{-c})\) with \(c\ge1/2\). Put \(q_n=\min\{1/4,n^{-\lambda}\}\). Ignoring constants, the three displayed terms have exponents \(r_0(1-\lambda)\), \(c-\lambda\), and \(a+\lambda/(2\gamma)\), where \(r_0=(1+\alpha)/(2+\alpha)\). Therefore the optimized raw feasible exponent is \(\widetilde r_{feas}=\sup_{\lambda\ge0}\min\{r_0(1-\lambda),c-\lambda,a+\lambda/(2\gamma)\}\), with optimizer \(\lambda_\star\) given in lem:additive-clip-balance-exponent. The declared minimax-comparable exponent is \(r_{feas}=\min\{r_\star,\widetilde r_{feas}\}\). Since \(r_{feas}\le\widetilde r_{feas}\), the same estimator satisfies \(\sup_{P\in P_{class}}E_P R_P(\widehat\pi_n)\le Cn^{-r_{feas}}(\log n)^p\). Also \(r_{feas}\le r_\star\) by construction. At \(\gamma=0\), choose a fixed \(q\le\underline p\). The true clipping displacement vanishes, \(r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}/q=O(n^{-1/2})\), and the empirical term gives exponent \(r_0=(1+\alpha)/(2+\alpha)\). Because \(\beta_{\alpha,0}=0\), \(r_\star=r_0\), so \(r_{feas}=r_\star\).

This proves the narrowed rate-characterization statement proposed below. The lower bound is exactly thm:minimax-lower: under ass:margin-window and the policy-richness condition that the two witness-optimal policies belong to \(\Pi\), there is \(c>0\) such that \(M_n(\alpha,\gamma)\ge c n^{-r_\star(\alpha,\gamma)}\) for all large \(n\). For the upper bound, oeq:feasible-upper constructs the cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM with \(q_n=\min\{1/4,n^{-\lambda_\star}\}\) when \(\gamma>0\), and with fixed \(q\le\underline p\) when \(\gamma=0\), and proves \(\sup_{P\in P_{class}(\alpha,\gamma)}E_P R_P(\widehat\pi_n)\le Cn^{-r_{feas}(\alpha,\gamma)}(\log n)^p\) under ass:marginal-overlap, ass:nuisance-rate, ass:vc-localized-envelope, and the strict-overlap endpoint at \(\gamma=0\). Since \(M_n\) is the infimum over all measurable \(\Pi\)-valued estimators, it is bounded above by the risk of this particular estimator. Combining the two inequalities yields \(c n^{-r_\star}\le M_n(\alpha,\gamma)\le Cn^{-r_{feas}}(\log n)^p\). The corrected definition sets \(r_{feas}=\min\{r_\star,\widetilde r_{feas}\}\), hence \(r_{feas}\le r_\star\). At \(\gamma=0\), \(\beta_{\alpha,0}=0\) and oeq:feasible-upper gives \(r_{feas}=r_\star=(1+\alpha)/(2+\alpha)\). For \(\gamma>0\), the gap is positive exactly in the nuisance-limited subregime \(\widetilde r_{feas}<r_\star\); otherwise the high-level nuisance-rate calculation closes the bracket at the converse exponent.

added_lemmas.statement: For \(q\in(0,1/2]\), any measurable plug-in nuisance triple \(\bar\eta=(\bar\mu_0,\bar\mu_1,\bar e)\), and \(b_q(x)=E_P[\Gamma_q(O;\bar\eta)\mid X=x]-\tau_P(x)\), every deterministic policy \(\pi\) satisfies \(|E_P[(\pi(X)-\pi_\star(X))b_q(X)]|\le q^{-1}\|\bar e_q-e_P\|_2(\|\Delta_1\|_2+\|\Delta_0\|_2)\). Consequently, for \(\bar\eta=\widehat\eta\), ass:nuisance-rate and lem:true-clipped-propensity-envelope imply the uniform bound \(|E_P[(\pi-\pi_\star)b_q]|\le C\{r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}/q+1\{\gamma>0\}r_{\mu,n}q^{1/(2\gamma)}\}\), with the last term absent at \(\gamma=0\) under the strict-overlap endpoint and \(q\le\underline p\).
added_lemmas.proof_tex: By lem:clip-bias, \(b_q=(\bar e_q-e_P)(\Delta_1/\bar e_q+\Delta_0/(1-\bar e_q))\). Since \(q\le \bar e_q\le1-q\), both denominators are at least \(q\). Also \(|\pi-\pi_\star|\le1\). Hence Cauchy-Schwarz over \(P_X\) gives \(|E[(\pi-\pi_\star)b_q]|\le E|b_q|\le q^{-1}\|\bar e_q-e_P\|_2(\|\Delta_1\|_2+\|\Delta_0\|_2)\). For \(\bar\eta=\widehat\eta\), ass:nuisance-rate gives \(\|\Delta_0\|_2+\|\Delta_1\|_2\le Cr_{\mu,n}\) and \(\|\widehat e-e_P\|_2\le r_{e,n}\). Lem:true-clipped-propensity-envelope gives \(\|\widehat e_q-e_P\|_2\le r_{e,n}+Cq^{1+1/(2\gamma)}\) for \(\gamma>0\), and \(\|\widehat e_q-e_P\|_2\le r_{e,n}\) for \(\gamma=0\) when \(q\le\underline p\). Substitution proves the stated bounds.

added_lemmas.statement: For the cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM and any \(q\in(0,1/2]\), \(E_P R_P(\widehat\pi_n)\le C\{[n^{-1/2}q^{-1/2}(\log n)^{p_0}]^\kappa+r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}/q+1\{\gamma>0\}r_{\mu,n}q^{1/(2\gamma)}\}\), where \(\kappa=2(1+\alpha)/(2+\alpha)\). At \(\gamma=0\), under ass:strict-overlap-endpoint and \(q\le\underline p\), the last term is absent.
added_lemmas.proof_tex: Condition on the nuisance-training folds. By the ERM property in def:feasible-erm, \(P_n[(\pi_\star-\widehat\pi_n)\Gamma_q(\widehat\eta)]\le0\). Adding and subtracting population means gives \(R_P(\widehat\pi_n)\le (P-P_n)[(\pi_\star-\widehat\pi_n)\Gamma_q(\widehat\eta)]+|P[(\pi_\star-\widehat\pi_n)b_q]|\). The clipped AIPW score has conditional second moment on a disagreement set \(D\) bounded by \(Cq^{-1}P_X(D)\), because outcomes and true regressions are bounded and the clipped denominators are at least \(q\). Assumption ass:vc-localized-envelope therefore bounds the expected localized empirical term by \(C n^{-1/2}q^{-1/2}P_X(D_{\widehat\pi_n})^{1/2}(\log n)^{p_0}\). Lem:feasible-drift-global-bound bounds the drift term by \(B_n(q)=C\{r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}/q+1\{\gamma>0\}r_{\mu,n}q^{1/(2\gamma)}\}\). By thm:margin-localization, \(P_X(D_{\widehat\pi_n})^{1/2}\le C R_P(\widehat\pi_n)^{\alpha/(2+2\alpha)}\). Thus \(R_P(\widehat\pi_n)\le C A_n(q)R_P(\widehat\pi_n)^s+B_n(q)\), where \(A_n(q)=n^{-1/2}q^{-1/2}(\log n)^{p_0}\) and \(s=\alpha/(2+2\alpha)\). If \(r\le ar^s+b\) with \(0\le s<1\), then either \(r\le 2b\) or \(r/2\le ar^s\), giving \(r\le (2a)^{1/(1-s)}+2b\). Since \(1/(1-s)=2(1+\alpha)/(2+\alpha)=\kappa\), the asserted bound follows after integrating over the training folds.

added_lemmas.statement: Let \(\gamma>0\), \(r_0=(1+\alpha)/(2+\alpha)\), and suppose \(r_{\mu,n}=O(n^{-a})\), \(r_{\mu,n}r_{e,n}=O(n^{-c})\) with \(c\ge1/2\). For the additive master bound, the polynomial clip \(q_n=\min\{1/4,n^{-\lambda_\star}\}\) has optimized raw exponent \(\widetilde r_{feas}=\sup_{\lambda\ge0}\min\{r_0(1-\lambda),c-\lambda,a+\lambda/(2\gamma)\}\). A closed-form optimizer is as follows. If \(a\ge\min\{r_0,c\}\), take \(\lambda_\star=0\) and \(\widetilde r_{feas}=\min\{r_0,c\}\). If \(a<\min\{r_0,c\}\) and either \(c<r_0\) or \(c<T_A\), take \(\lambda_\star=2\gamma(c-a)/(1+2\gamma)\) and \(\widetilde r_{feas}=(c+2\gamma a)/(1+2\gamma)\). If \(a<r_0\) and \(c\ge T_A\), take \(\lambda_\star=2\gamma(r_0-a)/(1+2\gamma r_0)\) and \(\widetilde r_{feas}=r_0(1+2\gamma a)/(1+2\gamma r_0)\), where \(T_A=[r_0(1+2\gamma a)+2\gamma(r_0-a)]/(1+2\gamma r_0)\).
added_lemmas.proof_tex: For \(q_n=n^{-\lambda}\), the three terms in lem:feasible-additive-master-bound have exponents \(A(\lambda)=r_0(1-\lambda)\), \(B(\lambda)=c-\lambda\), and \(C(\lambda)=a+\lambda/(2\gamma)\). The function \(\min\{A,B,C\}\) is the minimum of affine functions, so its maximum occurs at \(\lambda=0\) or where the increasing line \(C\) meets the lower envelope of the two decreasing lines \(A\) and \(B\). If \(a\ge\min\{r_0,c\}\), then \(C(0)\) is not below the decreasing envelope at the origin, so \(\lambda=0\) is optimal and the value is \(\min\{r_0,c\}\). Now assume \(a<\min\{r_0,c\}\). If \(c<r_0\), then \(B\) is already below \(A\) at the origin and remains below because it has the steeper negative slope, so the optimum is at \(B=C\), namely \(\lambda=2\gamma(c-a)/(1+2\gamma)\) and value \((c+2\gamma a)/(1+2\gamma)\). If \(c\ge r_0\), the lower decreasing envelope is first \(A\), then \(B\) after their crossing. The intersection \(A=C\) is \(\lambda=2\gamma(r_0-a)/(1+2\gamma r_0)\), with value \(r_0(1+2\gamma a)/(1+2\gamma r_0)\). This point is feasible before \(B\) becomes binding exactly when \(B\) at that point is at least this value, equivalently \(c\ge T_A=[r_0(1+2\gamma a)+2\gamma(r_0-a)]/(1+2\gamma r_0)\). In that case it is optimal. If \(c<T_A\), \(B\) becomes binding first, and the optimum is the \(B=C\) value just computed. These cases exhaust all possibilities.

proposed_statement_changes.current: Under ass:margin-window, ass:marginal-overlap, ass:nuisance-rate, Pi rich (witness-optimal policies in Pi), gamma<=alpha+1: c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma)} (log n)^p, with r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}) (sharp converse, thm:minimax-lower, all gamma) and r_feas the DERIVED plain-clipped-AIPW exponent (oeq:feasible-upper), r_feas<=r_star with equality at gamma=0. FIRST welfare-regret rate under weak (one-sided polynomial) overlap, extending strict-overlap policy learning. The r_star-r_feas gap (positive for gamma>0) is the weak-overlap nuisance-estimation cost; tightness is open (oeq:feasible-tight).
proposed_statement_changes.proposed: Under ass:margin-window, ass:marginal-overlap, ass:nuisance-rate, ass:vc-localized-envelope, Pi rich (witness-optimal policies in Pi), and gamma<=alpha+1, the corrected feasible-rate definition gives c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma)} (log n)^p, with r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}) and r_feas=min{r_star, r_tilde_feas}. Here r_tilde_feas is the optimized additive clipped-AIPW exponent from oeq:feasible-upper. Thus r_feas<=r_star and equality holds at gamma=0; for gamma>0 the gap is positive exactly in the nuisance-limited subregime r_tilde_feas<r_star, while the remaining tightness question is recorded in oeq:feasible-tight.
proposed_statement_changes.reason: The original qualitative clause says the gap is positive for every gamma>0. The derived exponent depends on the declared nuisance exponents a and c. If those high-level nuisance rates are fast enough, the capped bracket exponent equals r_star even when gamma>0. The truth-preserving narrowing keeps the proved two-sided bracket and replaces the universal positive-gap claim by the exact subregime condition.

proposed_definition_changes.current: Let kappa=2(1+alpha)/(2+alpha). For gamma>0 and certified polynomial nuisance exponents a,c satisfying r_mu,n=O(n^{-a}) and r_mu,n r_e,n=O(n^{-c}), c>=1/2, define zeta(a,c,gamma)=sup_{lambda>=0} min{1/2-lambda/2,c-lambda,a+lambda/(2gamma)} with the closed-form optimizer in lem:clip-balance-exponent. Define r_plug=kappa zeta(a,c,gamma), q_n=n^{-lambda_*} capped at 1/4, and r_feas=min{r_star(alpha,gamma),r_plug}. For gamma=0 under a strict-overlap floor, set q_n to a fixed value below the floor and r_feas=r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha).
proposed_definition_changes.proposed: Let r0=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha). For gamma>0 and certified polynomial nuisance exponents a,c satisfying r_mu,n=O(n^{-a}) and r_mu,n r_e,n=O(n^{-c}), c>=1/2, define the additive feasible exponent r_tilde_feas(a,c,gamma)=sup_{lambda>=0} min{r0(1-lambda), c-lambda, a+lambda/(2gamma)}. Let lambda_* be the closed-form optimizer in lem:additive-clip-balance-exponent and set q_n=min{1/4,n^{-lambda_*}}. Define r_feas(alpha,gamma,a,c)=min{r_star(alpha,gamma), r_tilde_feas(a,c,gamma)}. For gamma=0 under a strict-overlap floor, set q_n to a fixed value below the floor and r_feas=r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha).
proposed_definition_changes.reason: The exact drift identity in lem:clip-bias gives a global second-order feasible drift term. Under the frozen L2 nuisance assumptions this drift enters the basic regret inequality additively, while only the empirical-process term is exponentiated by the margin self-bound. Therefore the old formula kappa*min{1/2-lambda/2,c-lambda,a+lambda/(2gamma)} overstates the nuisance-bias part of the feasible rate. The corrected definition uses the actual exponents of the additive master bound: the empirical exponent is r0(1-lambda), while the two drift exponents remain c-lambda and a+lambda/(2gamma).

open_obligations.what_is_open: Determine whether the feasible clipped-AIPW ERM, or any genuinely feasible estimator, can attain the converse exponent r_star(alpha,gamma) throughout the weak-overlap class when the derived raw exponent r_tilde_feas is below r_star.
open_obligations.obstruction: The solved upper bound is driven by a global L2 second-order drift term from lem:clip-bias. The frozen assumptions do not give a sharper localization of the product (ehat_q-e_P)(muhat-mu) on the policy disagreement set, nor do they prove a feasible nuisance estimator whose weak-arm regression error uniformly matches the lower-bound calibration. Without such a new idea, the bracket cannot be closed in the nuisance-limited weak-overlap subregime.
open_obligations.attempted: The proof used the exact clipped-score identity, the true-clipping envelope from ass:marginal-overlap, the localized VC empirical-process envelope, and an optimized polynomial clipping schedule. A tempting stronger route would put the drift inside the margin self-bound as a P_X(D_pi)^{1/2}-localized term, but that would require an L2 bound on the pointwise product score error or an equivalent localized nuisance condition not supplied by the frozen core.
open_obligations.partial_result: The current solve proves the honest feasible bracket: thm:minimax-lower gives M_n(alpha,gamma)>=c n^{-r_star}; oeq:feasible-upper gives the explicit feasible estimator bound sup_P E R_P(pi_hat_n)<=C n^{-r_feas}(log n)^p with r_feas=min{r_star, sup_lambda min(r0(1-lambda),c-lambda,a+lambda/(2gamma))} for gamma>0 and r_feas=r_star at gamma=0. Tightness is open only where the raw feasible exponent is strictly below r_star.
